notes: The 2022 UCC amendments introduce a new Article 12 on "controllable electronic records," with Section 12-105 defining "control" consistently with DAPL Principle 6 and Section 12-105(d) specifically being compatible with DALP Principle 7.
Our approach to control aligns with Principle 6 of UNIDROIT’s Principles on Digital Assets and Private Law"  CONSULTATION PAPER NO. 4 September 2023

Principles reflected are Principle 8 (innocent acquisition) and a limited reference to Principle 6 (control). Decree-Law No. 25 of 17 March 2023 was drafted with the DAPL Principles in mind. Article 5(4) reflects the innocent acquisition rule by protecting a good-faith acquirer who obtains an entry in the digital circulation register on the basis of a suitable title, while Article 17 refers to control of the means of access to digital financial instruments, though in a regulatory rather than private-law sense.

The Digital Assets (Scotland) Act 2026 is consistent with several core aspects of the DAPL Principles, particularly Principle 3(1) on digital assets as objects of proprietary rights, Principle 6 on control, and Principle 8 on innocent acquisition.  The Policy Memorandum cites the UNIDROIT Principles, noting that "a technology neutral approach to defining digital assets aligns with that adopted in cross-jurisdictional statements of relevant private law principles, notably The International Institute for the Unification of Private Law ('UNIDROIT') Principles on Digital Assets and Private Law".
